prompt: I want a login page for a SaaS product. Show me 3 contrasting style directions.
expected: Trigger clarifying questions about design context and brand; produce 3 design_canvas variations; avoid AI slop such as purple gradients, emoji, and Inter; explain clearly which dimension each variation changes and why.
tests: workflow questioning + variation logic + anti-AI slop checklist + design_canvas usage

prompt: Help me create a 10-slide product pitch deck for an AI startup project.
expected: Start from deck_stage.js; first vocalize the design system (color, type, layout rhythm) and get confirmation; alternate among section divider, content, data, and quote layouts; type size >= 24px; 1-indexed labels.
tests: Junior Designer reports first, then builds + deck_stage usage + visual rhythm + scale standards

prompt: Create a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work.
expected: Use Stage+Sprite from animations.jsx; write the timeline before writing components; use easeOut for entrances and easeIn for exits; tell the story in phases instead of stacking random animations; keep text on screen for at least 3 seconds.
tests: animation workflow + correct easing + rhythm design + duration control

prompt: Create a Habit Tracker app prototype.
expected: Ask whether the user wants an overview spread or a flow demo (default to overview); use assets/ios_frame.jsx; do not hand-code the Dynamic Island; Tracker is a high-density product type, so each screen needs >= 3 information-density elements (habit completion rate, streak, trend chart, achievement badge, etc., not decoration); show at least 5-7 screens side by side (home / create habit / detail / stats / settings).
tests: overview/flow routing + hard binding to ios_frame + information-density classification (high density) + multi-screen side-by-side layout

prompt: Create a reading notes app prototype.
expected: Default to an overview spread; use ios_frame.jsx; reading notes is more content-display-oriented, so the density requirement is less extreme than Tracker, but the notes list page still needs >= 3 layers of information (book, quote, tag, progress); show at least 4-6 screens (bookshelf home / note detail / highlight annotation / search / notebook management); prefer a serif display typeface.
tests: overview by default + ios_frame + information hierarchy + content-led visual rhythm

prompt: Create a running tracker app prototype.
expected: Use an overview spread; use ios_frame.jsx; a running app is high density (map, pace chart, heart-rate zones, per-kilometer splits), so each screen needs >= 3 product-differentiating information areas; show at least 5 screens (today overview / live run data / route map / history / monthly stats); avoid AI slop collisions (no purple gradients, no decorative icon overload, but icons inside data visualizations are acceptable).
tests: overview + ios_frame + high-density data visualization + mixed map/chart layouts + slop edge cases
